master_row.title: Helicity Topology Invariant
master_row.canonical_statement: This theorem (Calugareanu--White--Fuller) states that the total helicity of intertwined structures decomposes into geometric writhe and internal twist.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation relating a, u, L, m, b.
master_row.dictionary: The linking number of two closed curves Lk(C1,C2) equals the sum of their writhe and twist: Lk = Wr + Tw, and this quantity remains invariant under continuous deformations of the curves【315394840893214†L423-L431】.
master_row.encyclopedia: This theorem (Călugăreanu–White–Fuller) states that the total helicity of intertwined structures decomposes into geometric writhe and internal twist. In the TZP context, it provides a topological conservation law for magnetic and fluid helicity. Interpretive role: This entry specifies the structure within the registry.
